def: s.m. Injury, pain, rack, torment, torture.
more: n. f. The leather thong of a bucket.

ety: kas˚ = S. कष्, or कृष्; ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cause to assay, or test, or try, or prove; to have (a thing) tested, &c.
more: 1. v. a. (causal of کسنا from कष A touchstone, or r. कुष Assay, or r. कृष Draw) To cause to try, prove or assay. 2. To cause to tighten. 3. v. n. To be spoiled (curds, &c.) by standing in a metal vessel.

ety: kas˚ = S. कंस+ānā, as in kasānā, 'to test,' q.v.
def: v.n. To acquire a metallic taste, to be spoiled (curds, &c.) by standing in a metallic vessel.
v.t. To cause to tighten; — to tie tight, to bind ( = kaswānā).
s.f. A kind of poisonous spider.
s. (dakh.) Bell-metal ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
attractive, good-looking; beautiful; pleasant, agreeable; excellent, superb
wety: From Classical Persian قَشَنْگ (qašang, “beautiful, lovely”), ultimately from Sogdian ʾγšnk- ((ə)xšank, “beautiful”).

def: s.m. Tightening; tightness ( = kasāwaṭ, kasāʼī); astringency.
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat (syn. laḍḍū).
more: s. m. A kind of sweetmeat.
s. m. (dakh.) Brasier, founder (of metal, Harris ), maker of metal pots: see کسيرا kaserā.

def: s.m. = kaserā, q.v. s.m. A vendor of metallic vessels; a brasier, pewterer, coppersmith, plumber
subs: or kaser-haṭ (kaser, for kaserā), s.m. Brasiers' quarter.

def: adj. Tightening; astringent.
s.f. A kind of pickle or chutnee made of oil; — the plant Cassia sophera.
more: n. f. 1. A plant; Cassia sophora , Wat. 2. An oil pickle.
s. f. 1. A kind of pickle. 2. Name of a plant. (Cassia sophera).

def: s.f. A touchstone; — assay; test, proof
subs: or kasnā, or lagānā, v.t. To apply to or to rub upon, the touchstone, to test, prove.

def: n. f. S. कष. 1. A touchstone; test. 2. Proof, assay; criterion. kasauṭī par chaṛhānā, lagānā, yā kasnā , See آزمانا 1. 2. Ādmī ke haq meṅ muāmlah kasautī hai . Prov. Dealings with a man are the touchstone by which he is known.
s.m. (dialec.), A handkerchief tied round the head (on going to bed).
more: s. (dakh.) A handkerchief tied round the head on going to bed ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

more: (from قسا Heb. To be hard) s. f. Hardness of heart, grief, anguish, pain, chagrin.

ety: kasā(nā)+āwaṭ or āhaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Tightening; tightness; — an astringent.
n. f. Tightening; tightness. Laqā kī kasāvaṭ hī kā mol hai .
adj. Ready, tied up.

ety: inf. n. of کسب 'to acquire, gain, earn,' &c.
def: s.m. Acquirement, acquisition (by labour), earning, gain; industry, employment, occupation, trade, profession, handicraft; art, skill; prostitution, harlotry
more: n. m. 1. Profession; occupation ( peshā ). 2. Art; skill. 3. Prostitution; whoredom. kasab k., yā kamāānā , v. a. To lead the life of a prostitute.
s. m. 1. Trade, profession, employment. 2. Gain, acquirement, acquisition (by labour).
subs: To acquire, gain, earn; to follow a trade or profession; — to practise prostitution, lead the life of a harlot.

ety: for A. قصبة qaṣabat, n. of un. fr. qaṣab; rt. قصب 'to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. A large village; a small town (particularly when inhabited by decent people or families of some rank); a township.
more: n. m. pl. qasbāt . A town or large village.
(prop. ḳaṣaba, from قصب) s. m. A small town (particularly, when inhabited by decent people or families of rank).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قصبه (qasba), from Arabic قَصْبَة (qaṣba).

def: (for A. qaṣabāt, pl. of qaṣabat), s.m. pl. Towns; large villages.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a large village, or a town; rustic; boorish; — a villager; a townsman.
more: adj. Belonging to the town; boorish. qasbātī , n. m. A townsman; a villager. qasbāt'ī , adj. Belonging to the town. qasbātī madrasā . A town or middle school.

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
s.m. Designation of the class which works in bell-metal (kāṅsā); an individual of that class, a worker in bell-metal (cf. kaserā).
s.m. A weaver.
s.m. An artisan, artificer; a trader; — s.f. A prostitute, harlot, courtezan
more: n. f. A woman of the town ( besvā , 1). Kasbī ke bāp/?/kā nām kyā ? Prov. Who is the harlot's father?
s. m. 1. An artisan, artificer. 2. s. f. A prostitute, courtezan.
subs: s.m. A house of ill-fame, a brothel.

def: s.f. A prostitute ( = kasbī).
s.f. A prostitute ( = kasbī).
oblique plural of قَصْبَہ (qasba, “town”)

ety: inf. n. of قصد 'to intend; to propose to oneself,' &c.
def: s.m. Intention, design, purpose, resolve, aim, object; project, attempt, endeavour; desire, wish, will, inclination
more: n. m. See اراده 2-5 Attempt; project. qasd'an , adv. 1. Wilfully ( irādatan ). 2. In cold blood. qasd'an iānat k . G. G. To intentionally aid. qasd k . v. a. See irāda k . To attempt; essay; strive; make an attempt; try one's hand; venture.

s. m. Intention, design, aim, object, desire, wish, inclination, purpose, project, attempt, conspiracy. قصد کرنا ḳaṣd karnā, To design, intend, project, propose to oneʼs self, resolve, proceed, journey (towards), to make oneʼs object or aim, to make an attempt. قصد مصمم ḳaṣd-i-muṣammam, Fixed resolution, firm intention.
subs: (-kā), To entertain the design (of); to harbour designs (against)

(-kā), To form the design (of), to propose to oneself; to intend, design, purpose, resolve, &c.; to endeavour, strive; to attempt, make an attempt (at or on); to make (a thing or a person) one's object or aim, to aim (at); to pursue; to make (for), journey (towards); to strike (at), form a design (against), to attack, to venture (on)

def: adv. Intentionally, purposely; expressly; voluntarily; wilfully, deliberately.
pron. Somebody, someone.
more: pron. Interrog. sing. कस्मै To whom; to what; to which.

def: part. (from کسنا) Tied up, prepared, ready.
oblique of kasā-kasāyā, q.v.s.v. kasā.
s.m. A vendor of metallic vessels; a brasier, pewterer, coppersmith, plumber
more: n. m. 1. One who sells metallic vessels; a brasier. 2. Grey cattle. kaser-haṭṭā, kaser-haṭ, kasar-haṭṭā . Brasiers' quarter.
s. m. A brasier or pewterer.
subs: or kaser-haṭ (kaser, for kaserā), s.m. Brasiers' quarter.

def: s.f. Wife of a brasier.
s.m. A kind of grass, Scirpus Kysoor; — the root of Cyperus tuberosus (which is eaten as a fruit).
more: n. m. S. कशेरु A root eaten as a fruit; cyperus tuberosus , Speede. kaserū sā sir , n. m. A bald head; a head with but few hairs.
s. m. A root so called. (Cyperus tuberosus: Willd. ).

def: s.m. See kaserū.
s.m. (Wind) breaking, or dashing to pieces (a ship, &c.); blowing with a loud noise (wind), thundering.
more: (Heb. To boil up, to foam, to rage) s. m. 1. The wind breaking, dashing to pieces (a ship). 2. Blowing with a loud noise (wind), thundering. ¶ ḳaṣif, adj. Weak, broken, split.

def: s.m. A crowd, a multitude.
oblique plural of کَشْف (kaśf)
vocative plural of کَشْف (kaśf)
Kashghar (a city)
s.m. A plasterer; a potter.
more: (for کاسه گر) s. m. 1. A plasterer. 2. Potter.

def: s.f. The eye-brow; — a slice, cut (syn. phāṅk), piece, bit; a chop, steak; — the bow or pommel (of a saddle, e.g. zīn-kā qāsh)
more: n. f. A slice ( phāṅk ). Ām kī qāsheṅ. qāsh qāsh k. , v. a. To slice. zīn kā qāsh . The bow or pommel of a saddle.
s. f. m. 1. The eyebrow; (hence) 2. A bit, a piece, a slice. زين کا قاش zīn kā ḳāsh, The bow or pommel of a saddle.
subs: v.t. To slice, to cut in pieces.

def: conj. Would to heaven, would that, I wish to heaven
more: adv. Would to God, God grant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کاش (kāš).

more: n. m. 1. ( In Comp .) Pulling. 2. One pull of a huqqā ( dam , 4). Ek kash lugā lūṅ to chalūṅ .
part. act. (of کشيدن) in compos. Drawing, drawer, pulling, carrying, bearing, suffering, enduring, &c. ¶ kush, part. act. (of کشتن) in compos. Killing, killer.

ety: rt. of kashīdan; Zend karesh = S. कर्ष
def: part. & s.m. Drawing, pulling; carrying, bearing, enduring; drawer; bearer, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. měḥnat-kash, 'enduring (or one who endures) affliction'); — a pull, a whiff (of a ḥuqqa, &c.)
subs: or kash-ā-kashī, or kash-ma-kash, s.f. Repeated pulling; pulling backwards and forwards, or to and fro; jostling, hustling; bringing and taking away; command after command; commanding and countermanding; great unpleasantness, or grief, or pain; distraction, dilemma, perplexity, difficulty; struggle, contention, wrangle, squabble; attraction, allurement (cf. aiṅć-ā-tānī).

def: s.m. A tortoise; the sign Cancer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَشْف (kašf), borrowed from Arabic كَشْف (kašf). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi کشف (kśf /⁠kaśf⁠/).

ety: kash, q.v.+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.f. A line, streak, stripe.
s.f. See s.v. kash. s.f. The eye-brow; — a slice, cut (syn. phāṅk), piece, bit; a chop, steak; — the bow or pommel (of a saddle, e.g. zīn-kā qāsh)
more: s. f. Repeated pulling; command after command, bringing and taking away, commanding and countermanding; great unpleasantness, grief or pain; pleasant and unpleasant, distraction, dilemma, perplexity, difficulty, contention, struggle, attraction, allurement.
subs: v.t. To slice, to cut in pieces.

def: s. m. A watchman, guard, porter.
s.m. A watchman, guard; porter.
s.m. Testing gold by the touchstone; testing, trying; test (of gold, &c.), trial, proof.

ety: imperf. part. of kashīdan; see kash
def: part. Drawing, pulling, attracting; withdrawing; bearing, suffering, enduring.
more: part. act. (of کشيدن) Drawing, withdrawing, bearing, suffering.

wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa). Doublet of کرشن (kirśn) and کنھیا (kanhaiyā).

def: s.m. A small house; a house, dwelling, edifice.
s.m. (?), Fibres of the wild date, or the palm tree; rope made of these fibres.
adj. Of or belonging to the city of Kāshān; — s.f. A fine kind of velvet.
s.f. = kasauṭī, q.v. s.f. A touchstone; — assay; test, proof
subs: or kasnā, or lagānā, v.t. To apply to or to rub upon, the touchstone, to test, prove.

def: s.f. An astringent ( = kasāwaṭ, q.v.). s.f. Hardness of heart; stinginess; — grief, anguish, affliction, anxiety, care, chagrin; melancholy, sadness.

s.m. Astringent flavour or taste; astringency; astringent juice; a decoction, infusion; ointment; gum, resin, extract, exudation (from a tree); the colour red; dirt, uncleanness, impurity; dulness, stupidity; stupor.

ety: inf. n. of کشف 'to open, reveal,' &c.
def: s.m. An opening, a declaration, solution, explanation; a manifestation, disclosure, apocalypse, revelation; inspiration.
more: n. m. 1. A manifestation; revelation. 2. Inspiration from heaven. Kashf o karāmāt hai .
s. m. An opening, a manifestation, revelation, declaration, solution, explanation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَشْف (kašf), borrowed from Arabic كَشْف (kašf). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi کشف (kśf /⁠kaśf⁠/).

def: adj. Disclosed, revealed, manifest.
s.f. Drawing; bearing, enduring, &c. (used as last member of compounds).
more: (in compos.) s. f. Drawing, bearing, enduring. ¶ kush-i, s. f. Killing.

def: s.f. Dragging, drawing, pulling; extracting; distilling.

ety: perf. part. of kashīdan; and = Zend karsta; S. कर्षित
def: part. adj. & s.m. Drawn, pulled, stretched, lengthened, prolonged, long, extended; contracted, pulled in; borne, endured, suffered, experienced; disturbed (in mind), vexed, displeased; sullen; supercilious; — having drawn; having endured, &c.; — a kind of needlework, embroidery
more: n. m. Needlework; embroidery. kashīda-khātir , adj. 1. Displeased; offended. 2 Distressed or distracted in mind. kashīdā kāṛhnā yā nikālnā , v. a. To do needlework. Āṅkh na dīda, kāṛhe kashīdā . Prov. Blind of eyes, needle plies!

(from کشيدن) 1. part. pass. Drawn, extended, contracted, pulled in, borne, endured, having drawn or endured. 2. s. m. A kind of needlework. 3. adj. Sullen, supercilious. کشيده خاطر kashīda-ḵẖāt̤ir, adj. Displeased. کشيده قد kashīda-ḳadd, or کشيده قامت kashīda-ḳāmat, adj. Tall.
subs: adj. Disturbed or distracted in mind; displeased, offended
or kashīda-qāmat, adj. Tall of stature
or kashīda nikālnā (-meṅ?), To trace figures in needlework (on cloth), to embroider.

def: s.f. Tension; displeasure; misunderstanding; superciliousness.
more: n. f. P. kashīdan to draw. Displeasure; misunderstanding.
s. f. Displeasure, sullenness, superciliousness, tension.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کشیدگی (kašīdagī), equivalent to کشید (kaśīd) + ـگی (-gī).

def: v.t. To draw, &c.; drawing, dragging, pulling; extending, stretching; enduring, suffering (cf. kashīd, and kash).
more: verbal n. or inf. To draw, drawing, enduring, suffering.

def: adj. Fit to be drawn, &c.

ety: act. part. of کشف 'to open, reveal,' &c.
def: s.m. Detector, discoverer; revealer; explainer, expounder.
more: (part. act. of کشف) s. m. Detecter, discoverer, explainer, revealer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کاشف (kāšif), from Arabic كاشِف (kāšif).

def: s.f. A drawing; a pull; attraction; allurement; curve or sweep (of a letter in writing); lingering, tardiness, delay; trial, difficulty, pressure (e.g. kashish-ke din); discord, difference, misunderstanding
more: n. f. کشيدن to draw. 1. A line or curve (in writing). In dāëroṅ kī kashish achchhī nahīṅ hai . 2. ( Mech .) Attraction; gravitation. 3. Attraction; allurement. Dil kī kashish hai . 4. Tardiness; delay. Meṅh ne baṛī kashish kar rakkhī hai . 5. ( kashṭ ke din ) Trial; difficulty; hard times. Kashish ke din nikal gaē . 6. Discord; differences; misunderstandings. Yeh dono āpas meṅ kashish rakhte haiṅ. kashish uṭhānā , v. n. To be in difficulty or distress. kashish-i-zamīṅ , Gravitation of the earth. kashish-i-ām ; H. sarab khiṅchāo , ( Phys .) Universal attraction. kashish-i-maqnātīsī . Magnetism.
(from کشيدن) s. f. Drawing, attraction, allurement, pull. ¶ kushish (from کشتن) s. f. Killing, slaughter.
subs: To experience hardship, suffer distress, to be hard pressed, or in difficulties

def: s.m.(?) Barley; barley-water; a kind of thick pottage made of wheat flour or barley meal with sheep's milk, to which flesh or wheat is added; sour milk dried.
s.m. The sectarial mark made by the Hindūs on the forehead with sandal, &c. (syn. ṭīkā)
more: s. m. A mark made by the Hindūs on the forehead with sandal, &c.: see ؟يکا ṭīkā. قشقه دينا ḳashḳa denā, To put the ḳashḳa on the forehead.
subs: To apply the qashqa (to the forehead).

def: conj. = kāsh, q.v. s.f. The eye-brow; — a slice, cut (syn. phāṅk), piece, bit; a chop, steak; — the bow or pommel (of a saddle, e.g. zīn-kā qāsh)
more: May it happen! God send! would!
subs: v.t. To slice, to cut in pieces.

def: s.f. = kać-kol, q.v. s.f. A (beggar's) cup or bowl (usually the lower half of a broken pitcher); a wallet; — a miscellany; a common-place book; an album.
more: s. A beggarʼs cup, &c., see کچکول kachkol.

def: s.f. See s.v. kash. s.f. The eye-brow; — a slice, cut (syn. phāṅk), piece, bit; a chop, steak; — the bow or pommel (of a saddle, e.g. zīn-kā qāsh)
more: n. f. See eṅchā-tānī . 1. Pulling backwards and forwards; jostling; hustling. 2. Dilemma; distraction. Kis kashmakash se vahāṅ pahuṅche!
subs: v.t. To slice, to cut in pieces.

def: s. f. Pulling backwards and forwards; distraction, difficulty, want, penury, struggle, dilemma, &c. See کشاکش kash-ā-kash.
adj. & s.m. Very clear or explicit; — a detector, discoverer, looser, solver, explainer.
more: (adj. superl. of کشف) 1. s. m. A discoverer, solver, explainer. 2. adj. Very explicit or clear.

ety: v.n. fr. kāshtan; rt. Zend karesh = S. कर्स् (कृष्)
def: s.f. Ploughing, tilling, cultivating; tillage, cultivation (syn. jot; bo-jot); cultivated land, a cultivated tract; a field, a farm; holding, tenure (of land)
more: n. f. 1. ( kāshtkārī ) Cultivation ( bo-jot ). 2. Cultivated land or tract; field; farm. 3. Holding; tenure (of land). kāsht se dakhal uṭhānā, kāsht yā qabze se be- dakhal k . To oust from a holding. kāsht karānā , v. a. To farm out land. kāsht k. , v. a. To cultivate ( bonā , 1). kāsht meṅ lānā , v. a. To bring into cultivation; reclaim land. be-kāsht , Ped. for H. be-jot ; Tir. aphāṛ , adj. Uncultivated; fallow; waste.
subs: s.m. A tiller of the soil, a cultivator, an agriculturist, a farmer
v.t. (caus. of next), To have or get (land) tilled or cultivated; to farm out (land)
(-ko), To bring into cultivation; to reclaim (land)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کاشت (kāšt, “farm”).

def: s.m. Evil; difficulty; bad state of things; penury, want, misery, wretchedness; pain, uneasiness, affliction, acuteness of pain, agony, suffering, distress, hardship; vehemence, violence; inconvenience, trouble, labour, effort, pains
more: s. m. Affliction, pain, distress, hardship, want, penury, vehemence, violence, acuteness (of pain), agony, difficulty.
subs: or kashṭ pānā, To suffer pain or distress, &c.
(-ko), To afflict, trouble, molest, persecute, hurt
adj. Accomplishable with effort or difficulty
To make an effort, to take pains or trouble.

ety: perf. part. of kāshtan; see kāsht
def: part. Tilled, cultivated; sown.

s.m. Wood, timber; a piece of wood

adj. Wooden; having wood.
s.f. Test, trial; pain, trouble.

(prop. kashtī ) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. A ship, vessel, bark, boat, ark. 2. A tray. 3. A beggarʼs plate or pot. ¶ kushtī, s. f. Wrestling. کشتي گير kushtī-gīr, or کشتي باز kushtī-bāz, or کشتي گر kushtī-gar, A wrestler. کشتي کرنا kushtī karnā, To wrestle, struggle.

To ply or to row a boat.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کشتی (kaštī, kištī).

def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Troubled; tried, tested.
n. m. A cultivator; farmer ( jotā ). kāsht-kārī ; H. kisānī , n. f. Culture; tillage.
s.f. = کاشي kāśī, q.v.
s.f. The modern city of Benares (the most sacred of all the cities of the Hindūs, and their most important place of pilgrimage)
more: s. f. A name of the city Benares.

(part. act. of قصد) s. m. A courier, an express, a messenger, envoy, postman.

(from قصد) s. m. A poem, a long ode.

adj. Of or relating to a courier, &c.; — s.f. Travelling or going as courier, the office or situation of courier.

def: v.t. To draw tight, to tighten, to brace; to gird up; to tie, fasten, strap, bind; to press upon, bear hard upon; to raise or increase (in price); to load (a gun); to dry up (the water, or juice, of); to fry (in ghī)

v. a. S. कृष 1. To tie; fasten; strap; bind. 1. Mā! ḍolī ḍanḍā kasā dharā, Maiṅ nahīṅ jātā rī, merī māṅ! Hin. Girls' song. The ḍoli , mother's ready got for me, I cannot, will not, mother go from thee! 2. Kātūṅgī moṭā moṭā sūt, bāṭāūṅgī jevṛā, Tale bāṅdhūṅ saut, ūpar bāṅdhūṅ bālmā, Kas kas bāṅdhūṅ saut, ḍhīle bāṅdhāṅ balmā! Wom. song. Such stout thread I will spin, and a stout rope to my mind, My co-wife underneath, my spouse above I'll bind, My co-wife oh! so tight! my husband loose I'il bind! 2. To make tight; brace up. 3. To press, or bear hard upon. 4. (S. कष try, test). To prove ( āzmānā , 1, 2). Sonā jāne kase, aur ādmī jāne base . Prov. The gold to prove touchstone apply, a man to know just keep him nigh. 5. To fry; dry up (the water, juice, etc). Kundā kasnā . 6. To load a gun. Bandūq kasnā . 7. To enhance the price. Dāmoṅ meṅ aur do paise kas dīye. kasnā , n. m. 1. Braces. 2. Be…
1. v. a. To tighten, tie, brace. 2. To assay, to try, to prove, examine. 3. To fry in melted butter. 4. s. m. A bundle, a wallet.

def: (dakh.) To decrease, to deduct.

s.f. Breaking; breach; routing, putting to flight; rout; (in Arith.) a fraction; — loss, damage; affliction, trouble, inconvenience; contrition; (in Gram.) the sign (ـِ) of the short vowel i; — (in Hindūstānī, perhaps by mistake for qaṣr, q.v.), something wrong or amiss; something wanting, a want; deficiency, defect, flaw
more: n. f. pl. کسور & کسزات from کسر breaking. ( Arith .) A fraction (S. bhinn ). 2. Damage; loss. Sawrupaë kī kasar paṛī . 3. Want ( ṭoṭā 5). 4. Defect; flaw. 5. Indigestion. Peṭ meṅ kasar hai. kasar bharnā , v. n. To indemnify ( toṭā uṭhānā , 2); to make up a deficiency. kasar beshī . A fraction more. kasar paṛnā , v. a. To come short; fail. Jo kisī bāt meṅ kasar paṛe to mujhe ulāhnā denā! kasar denā , v. a. To make one suffer a loss. kasar rakhnā , v. a. 1. To leave incomplete or unfinished. 2. To be wanting. kasar rahnā , v. n. To be deficient, in complete. kasar-i-shān , Loss of dignity or honor. kasar gair-vājīb ; A. H. dikhāū-kasar ; S. बिषमभिन्न ( Arith .) An improper fraction. kasar k. , v. a. To omit; fail; fall short. Tum ne kaun sī kasar kī thī? kasar khānā, yā uṭhānā , v. a. To suffer loss or damage. kasar kī bāṅṭ ; A. shumār kunindah ; S. अंश ( Arith .) The numerator. kasar ghaṭ…

s. f. 1. Breaking, breach, loss, affliction, a fraction (in arithmetick). 2. Rout, putting to flight. 3. The vowel (ِ) sounded like the Ital. short i, or the English i in bill, fin. (-meṅ?), To make a deduction (in), to deduct, to decrease; — to fall short (of), to fail, to be wanting (in)

or nikāl-lenā (-kī), To make up a loss or deficiency; to be revenged (for), be quits (with, -se), to pay (one) off (for)

The musk deer hunts perplexed, the perfume in his navel stored, So man without some holy guide in pain and doubt his Lord. ☞ Tradition represents the musk-deer as hunting about to find the odour that emanates from his own body, and the 2nd line refers to the Oriental doctrine of the indwelling, of the Divinity in man, and the search thereof ending in the return to self in the aham Birham or anal Haq , the eureka of the S fis . 2. (-se), To be cut, or cut off (by); to be cut short; to be settled, or adjusted; to be deducted (from); to be traversed or passed over (a road, &c.); to be performed or accomplished (a journey, &c.).

def: n. m. S. कुट्ट or कृत to cut. 1. See ṭukṛā (1, 2, 3). 2. A cut; slash; incision; bite; wound. 3. Cleavage ( chīr , 1). 4. A chip. 5. Section. 6. A passage; way; channel. 7. Separation; disconnection. 8. Dissection. 9. Corrosiveness; virulence. 10. The edge or sharp end. 11. Damage; loss. kāṭ phāṅs , n. f. 1. See kāṭ chhāṅṭ (5, 6). 2. Backbiting ( burāī , 2, 3). kāṭ phāṅs k. , v. a. 1. To cut and clip. 2. To backbite. kāṭ chhāṅṭ, kāṭ kūṭ , n. f. 1. Cutting and hacking ( chīr phāṛ ). 2. Cutting and clipping; paring. 3. Pruning; curtailing. 4. Scrapings; pickings. 5. Cuttings; deductions; retrenchments. 6. Petty savings; cheese-paring. 7. Scratching; scribbling. 8. Amendments ( tarāsh kharāsh ). kāṭ chhāṅṭ yā kāṭ kūṭ k. , v. a. 1. To cut off; cut out; amputate. 2. To prune ( chhāṅṭnā , 1, 5). 3. To hack; mangle; mutilate. 4. To make erasures; correct ( tarāsh kharāsh k. , 1). 5. To deduct; make deductions. Are pūrā nāmāṅ dīyā, ak kimaiṅ kāt kūṭ karī? Rus. Has he given you the full amount, or has he deducted any portion of it? kāṭ ḍālnā , v. a. To cut off; lop off. kāṭ-kūṭ yā aṅg biddyā ; A. ilm-i-tashrīh , ( Med ). Anatomy. (surgical). kāṭ kūṭ ke mutٴaalliq, kāṭ kūṭ kā , Anatomical; kāṭ khānā , v. a. To bite ( ḍasnā, buṛkā mārnā ). kāṭ khānā , v. n. To suffer loss or damage. kāṭ khāne ko dauṛnā , v. a. 1. To fly at one, as a dog. 2. To look fierce; speak harshly. Phr. To show one's teeth; to stand on one's hind legs. Bāt karte ho, yā kāt khāne ko dauṛte ho? kāṭ yā kaṭāo k. , v. a. 1. To cut a passage (for water). 2. ( kāṭ k .,) To wound; cut. 3. To cut away; corrode; destroy. Is marham ne baṛā kaṭāo kīyā! kāṭā , n. m. A bite. Sāṅp ke kāṭe kī davā. kāṭā, kāṭā huā , adj. Bitten. Sāṅp kā kāṭā pānī nahīṅ māṅgtā . Prov. The snake-bitten no water needs. (It is instant death). adh kāṭ S. व्यास ( Geom .) The diameter of a circle.

s.f. A cutting; cleavage; dissection; separation, severance, disconnection; a cut, slash, incision; execution; a bite; a wound; (met.) an untruth, a falsehood; — a section; a chip; a slice, piece, bit, part, fraction, patch, plot (of ground); — a passage, way, channel; — spur (of a cock); — scum, dirt, lees, sediment; — keenness, sharpness; edge (of a sword, &c.); — warmth; pungency; virulence; corrosiveness; damage, loss
more: s. f. 1. A cut, incision, execution. 2. Scum. 3. Virulence. کاٿ کرنا kāṭ karnā, v. a. To wound, to cut. کاٿ کوٿ kāṭ-kūṭ, s. f. Clippings, chips, scraps. کاٿ کوٿ کرنا kāṭ kūṭ karnā, 1. To clip, to cut out. 2. To deduct. کاٿ کهـانا kāṭ khānā, To bite.
s. (dakh.) 1. Brag, swaggering, bravado. 2. Spur (of a cock). کاٿ چلنا kāṭ chalnā, To brag, to swagger ( Harris ).
subs: s.f. Cutting and clipping; cuttings; deductions, &c. ( = kāṭ-ćhāṅṭ, and kāṭ-kūṭ); backbiting; — kāṭ-phāṅs karnā (-kī), To cut and clip; to backbite

or kāṭ-kūṭ, s.f. Cutting and clipping; cutting and hacking; pruning, lopping; cutting out; curtailing; scratching, scribbling; — cuttings, clippings, chips, scraps; deductions, retrenchments; scrapings, pickings; petty savings; erasures, amendments (in writing, &c.); — kāṭ-ćhāṅṭ karnā, or kāṭ-kūṭ karnā, v.t. To cut and clip; to prune; to cut out; to cut off; to amputate; to hack, mangle, mutilate; to scratch out, make erasures (in), to correct; to make deductions (from), to deduct

v.n. To make an incision or wound (in), to cut, to wound; to cut a passage (for); to cut away; to corrode; to destroy

s.f. The diameter (of a circle); the diagonal of a square).

s.m. Past time, the past; (in Gram.) the past tense; — adj. Of or relating to the past, retrospective

def: adv. Where? whither? what?
more: n. S. कुट्ट ( In comp .) Cutting. kaṭ-ret , n. m. ( Mech .) A rasp. kaṭ-kāl , adj. Restrospective. kaṭ-qabālah , n. Conditional mortgage; foreclosure of mortgage; conditional sale as to term, not as to time. kaṭ yā bītā kāl , n. 1. Past years. 2. ( Gram .) The past tense.
n. m. S. कुष्ट A medicine; Costus speciosus .

n. f. H. kuṭnā to pound. Stiffened paper, inserted between the cloth and lining of caps, etc.; buckram. Kuṭ kī topī hai. kuṭ-biddyā , n. f. Pommelling; chastisement. Tane ro lūṅ! are ā! terī kaisī kuṭ-biddyā karūṅ? Rus. Wom. The mother to her child. May I weep you dead! Come here just! I'll give you such a hammering!
1. ( कुत्र ) Where? whither? 2. ( कथं ) Why? how? ¶ kit ( कुत्र ) Where? whither? ¶ kit, How many? &c. See کتا kittā.

def: s.m. (dialec.), A knife for cutting bamboos with.
s.m. A bite ( = kāṭ).

def: part. adj. & s.m. Cut, cut off; bitten; reaped; spent, passed, &c.; — one who is bitten (by, -kā), &c.

ety: perf. part. of kaṭnā, and = S. कर्तित+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Cut, &c. (see kaṭnā)
more: s. f. Killing, slaughter. کٿا کرنا kaṭā karnā, To slaughter, murder.

def: s.m. What is spun; thread
more: n. m. S. कृत to cut. 1. W. What is spun; thread. 2. E. A ḍom's knife for cutting bamboos with. buṛ'hyā kā kātā , W. A sweetmeat made of thread like shreds of candied pumpkin.
subs: s.m. 'Old woman's spinning'; a kind of sweetmeat made of thread-like shreds of candied pumpkin.

s.f. Cutting and clipping; general retrenchment; — cutting and hacking, general slaughter or massacre
v.t. To cut up, to cut to pieces; to kill, murder, slaughter, massacre.

s.m. = kaṭāksh, q.v. s.m. Side-look, side-glance, leer, ogling
more: s. m. Ogling, leer, glance or side look.
subs: v.n. To cast a side-glance (at), to look askance (at); to leer; to ogle.

def: n. f. 1. Deadly enmity ( bair , 1). 2. Fight; pitched battle. Āj bheṛ māṅ Dūbaldhan ke kaṭāchhanī hovan lāgī , Hāth purkhan māṅ sel khābrī bījal jūṅ chamkan lāgī . Rus. In Dūbaldhan to-day they cut and slashed away, Their swords and spears like lightning flashed in fray.
s.m. A frying-pan, a shallow kind of boiler (for oil, butter, sugar, &c.), a caldron ( = kaṛāh, q.v.).
adj. = kaṭahā, and kaṭāsā, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Addicted to biting or snapping, snappish; — a biter (syn. kaṭṭar, kaṭkhanā).
adj. Snapping, inclined to bite, snappish.
adj. Inclined to bite; snappish; rabid. 1. Kaṭahā kuttā . 2. Yūṅ jī kāṭe sās kā tāna jūṅ kāṭe kūtā kaṭkhānā . Rus. Taunts of my mother-in-law as dog's bite sharp doth claw.
E. n. m. 1. Railings; palisade; bars. 2. The block on which fodder, etc. is cut ( neo ).
s.f. Spinning; price paid for spinning
more: n. f. 1. Spinning. 2. ( katvāī ) The price paid for spinning. katāī kar-ke khānā , v. n. To live by spinning thread. katāī k. , v. a. To spin.

n. f. Reaping (the harvest). kaṭāī kā samā, kaṭāī kī rut, kaṭāī kī sākh, kaṭnī , The reaping season; harvest. Bhāī! kaṭāī lage par to are chūn kā mānas bhī ṭhālī nāhīṅ rahe . My dear fellow! in the reaping season even a lay figure in flour would not be left unoccupied. kaṭāī k. , v. a. To cut the crop; to reap.

def: n. m. Linen. ☞ It is said to be so fine as to be rent by exposure to the moonlight, and hence it is represented by Oriental poets as enamoured of the moon.
adv. Cuttingly; conclusively, definitively, &c. ( = qat̤ʻī, q.v.); — in no shape or form, not at all; never.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قَطَعًا (qaṭaʕan).

def: s.m. A kind of cloth, fine linen (said to be so fine as to be rent by exposure to the moonlight; and hence, is represented by Oriental poets as enamoured of the moon).
v.t. To cause (one) to spin; to get spun ( = katwānā).
more: Caus. of kāṭnā . To cut or cause to cut or bite. 1. Khet kaṭāyā an-dātā pāyā . Prov. The field all mown, the grain's my own. 2. Are manne ke tat te khet kaṭānā saë? Rus. What! do I need your services to cut my corn? (I have no need of you). 3. Nadiyā kināre Mirjā koṭhavā uṭhaibo, ruchi ruchi kataibo khirakyā re kī . Mag. song. By riverside the Mirza's house uprose, With windows peeping out neat carved in rows.
(causal of کاتنا Sans. कर्त्तन ) v. a. To get spun, to cause to spin.

more: (caus. of کٿنا) v. a. To cut or cause to cut.

def: s.m. def: adj. & s.m. Cowardly, timorous, timid, faint-hearted, shrinking, irresolute; gentle; — discouraged; distressed, agitated; confused, perplexed; — a cowardly or faint-hearted person, &c.; — timidity, &c. ( = kātaratva, q.v.); — a large kind of fish, Cyprinus catla.
more: adj. Timid, timorous, confused, distressed, agitated with distress.

subs: s.m. One who carries a dagger, an assassin
or kaṭār mārnā (-ko), To put a dagger into (one), to stab.

def: s.m. A pole-cat ( = khaṭās, q.v.). s.f. Sourness, acidity ( = khaṭṭāpan, khaṭāʼī).

n. m. S. कट्टार A dagger; poniard; stilletto; dirk. ☞ A dagger having a broad straight blade the hilt of which comes up on either side of the wrist while it is grasped by a crossbar in the centre. 1. Karihaiyāṅ pe chale kaṭār, joban par tegā chale . Song. Her waist so slim makes falchions flash, o'er he/?/som's charms swords are drawn. 2. Koṭhe ūpar koṭhṛī sotī palaṅg bichhāë, Jad jāgūṅ jad ekli re! marūṅ kaṭārā khāë ! Dohā. Lone upon the housetop sleeping on my couch I lie, Lone I wake—ah! still alone! I'll stab myself and die. kaṭār bhoṅknā yā mārnā , v. a. To stab.
more: n. m. H. kū bad, ṭārī a horse. A hack.

more: n. f. S. कृत् to cut. A fillet.

(kā or -kī?) To cut out; to cut and clip, to retrench, to deduct (from); to pick and filch, to practise peculation, to purloin; — to plan, devise, contrive.

His sister is a tamarind sour, his mother a blade of steel, Himself a pit of deceit, dear! to whom can I appeal. 3. A medicinal plant; Globe thistle; Echinops echinatus . Roxb.

n. m. Cont. of کاتَهـ wood. kaṭh-bāp ; Sant. kākāt āpāt , n. m. A step-father. ☞ Women of the lower classes often leave, or are given up by their husbands, and on re-marrying ( karāo or dharāo ) they take with them the younger children to whom the new husband is kaṭh-bāp , while the children are called his ḍagarvā laṛke (picked up on the road or ḍagar ). kaṭh-birūkī, kath-meṅḍak , n. f. A toad. kaṭh-bandhan , n. m. A wooden ring to which the feet of elephants are fastened. kaṭh-bagṛā , n. m. A snipper; an ignorant tailor. kaṭh-baid , n. m. An ignorant practitioner; an empiric; a quack. kaṭh-bel ; Bhoj. chamarbelī , n. The acid wood-apple; Feronia elephantum. kaṭh-belā , n. m. Jasminum multiflorum. kaṭh-putlī , n. f. 1. A puppet; a toy. 2. A lean delicate girl. kaṭh putlī kā nāch , A puppet show; marionettes. kaṭh-phoṛā, khuṭak-baṛhaiyā, lakkaṛ phoṛā , W.; kaṭh-khudvā , E.; satraṅgyā , Br…

To pine away. 2. To be petrified with astonishment.

n. m. S. काष्ठ, 1. Wood; timber. 1. Bhāi! kāṭh kī roṭī peṭ ke bāṅdhoṅ bhūk nā ṭale . Rus. Prov. (Said by the creditor). My dear fellow! my hunger is not to be appeased by a wooden plate in lieu of bread. 2. Are bole nā! kyoṅ kāṭh-kā ban gayā? Rus. What, cant you speak? What makes you as mute as a post? 2. The stocks. Bhāī! Rām ne berāṅ-kar kāṭh sūṅ pāoṅ nikāsā saë . Rus. Friend! By the grace of Ram my legs are out of the stocks. kāṭh , adj. 1. Hard. 2. Cruel. kāṭh chabānā , v. a. lit . to chew wood. To fare ill. kāṭh kā ullū, kāṭh-kā , n. m. An arrant fool. kāṭh kā ghoṛā , n. m. lit . a wooden horse. 1. A lame man's stick; a crutch. 2. A bier. 1. Laṅgaṛ-dīn paune tīn, kāṭh kā ghoṛā, lohe kā zīn . Boys. The lame man see, legs a quarter under three, a horse of wood, an iron saddle good! 2. Bhāī! kāṭh ke ghoṛe kitai na chalte dekhe . Rus. My good fellow! you never yet saw a wooden horse run. (Tell that to the marines). kāṭh kabāṛ , n. m. 1. A promiscuous heap of broken furniture, etc. 2. Stale hard bread. kāṭh kaṭavval , n. m. A boy's game. One boy who has hold of a post leaves it by way of defiance to another boy who takes the part of a thief and tries to catch him before he can return to the post. If he succeeds his captive becomes the theif. kāṭh kī bhambo , n. f. 1. A puppet. 2. A ninny. kāṭh kī ghoṛī , n. f. lit . a wooden mare. A bier; a coffin. kāṭh meṅ pāoṅ ṭhoknā yā denā , v. a. To put in the stocks. kyāṅṭh meṅ ṭhoke daroggā, kaiṅ ne phāṅsī, de divā, Le jī! ke bhāje haiṅ ham Hulsan kā tambū pāṛ ke? Rus. Dalmīr. The gaoler's put me in the stocks. may be he'll hang me next, Just see, my friends! have I Ṭhug. Wilson robbed, his tent cut through? kāṭh h. , v. a. lit . to become like a stick. To be petrified with astonishment; frozen. kāṭh kī hāṅḍī , n. f. 1. A wooden pot. 2. Anything frail.
s.m. (in comp.) Wood; (it sometimes expresses 'excellence,' 'superiority,' 'excess,' &c.)

ety: S. क्कथः, or क्काथः; and क्कथ+ कः
def: s.m. An astringent vegetable extract (the produce of the Mimosa catechu) which the natives of India eat with betel leaf; catechu, terra japonica (syn. khair, q.v.).
more: s. m. The astringent vegetable extract, which the natives eat with betel-leaf. It is the produce of a species of Mimosa (chadira) catechu, terra japonica.

def: s.f. A word; speech, discourse; a story, tale, relation, recital, narrative, fable
more: n. f. S. कथ to speak. 1. A narrative (قصه 1). 2. Preaching; religious recital. 1. Maṅgal karnī, Kal-mal harnī, Tulsī! kathā Raghunāth . Tulsī Dās. Joy-inspiring, Kaljug foulness cleansing, O Tulsi! is the story of Raghunāth. 2. Kari parinām nij kathā sunāī, Rām kripā āpan gatī pāī . Rāmāyan. 3. Bhāī! kisāṅṛ ne ke dhaule latte pahar-kar kathā bāṅchnī saë? Rus. My dear fellow! What has a peasant to do with clean clothes? Is he going to preach? kathā-bārtā , n. m. Religious controversy. kathā-bāṅchnā, bakhānnā, yā kahnā , v. a. 1. To read or recite a sacred book. 2. To preach. kathā bāṅchne-vālā , n. m. A preacher. kathā biṭhānā , Hin. v. a. To entertain a reader. kathā sampūran h. , v. n. To be finished (a kathā , q. v).
s. f. A story, tale, fable, relation, narrative.
subs: or kathā bakhānnā, To read or recite a story, or a sacred book, &c.; to preach

more: n. S. कथक 1. A singer. 2. A dancing boy. 1. Batānā Katthakoṅ par khatam hai . In the art of representation the Katthak is perfection. 2. Nāchoṅ meṅ nāch Katthak kā! Prov. The Katthak's is the dance to see!

n. f. A wooden or stone vessel for kneading flour; a kneading trough; a small tub. 1. Rāṅpī, ārī, suī kaṭhautī, yehī chamāran kī saë roṭī. Jaë hai Gurū Rāë Dās kī! Chisel, needle. awl, and wooden cup the cobbler help to breakfast, dine, and sup. Glory be to Gurū Rāë Dās! (The cobbler's morning prayer before he sets to work). 2. Ek to mīṭh, dūsar kaṭhaut bhar . Sweet, and a bucket full! 3. Ath kaṭhautī māṭṭhā pīyeṅ, solah makunī khāeṅ Unke mare na roiye, ki ghar ke diliddar jāëṅ! Prov. Of butter milk eight jugs he drank, and sixteen cakes he ate, Weep not his death with whom the house doth poverty vacate!
